findings.description: Self-caught during round-2 review: src/assistant/core/graph_client.py:_get_bytes_inner allowed the 401-refresh path to fire AFTER a 302/307 redirect. Once we have stripped Authorization on redirect, a 401 from the redirect target does NOT mean our Graph bearer is stale; the previous code would have force-refreshed and re-attached the Graph bearer to a non-Graph host, leaking the token (D4 violation — same principle that motivates strip-Authorization on redirect). Fixed in this commit by adding `and redirect_follows == 0` to the 401-refresh guard. Reported here so the round-2 audit trail captures the catch.
findings.resolution: Already addressed in this commit. The 401-refresh path now requires redirect_follows == 0; after a redirect we never auth-refresh because we have left the Graph trust boundary.

findings.description: src/assistant/core/graph_client.py:_get_bytes_inner: redirect Location validation uses a startswith-check against `https://` rather than parsing the URL and asserting scheme=='https'. A Location header equal to `https://evil.com\nX-Inject: foo` would pass the startswith but is a malformed URL. httpx will likely reject the malformed URL when constructing the next request, so the practical impact is bounded — but a urlparse-based check would be tighter.
findings.resolution: Optional hardening: parse the Location with urlparse(), assert .scheme == 'https' and .netloc is non-empty. Defer to follow-up; current check is sufficient for the documented threat model.

findings.description: src/assistant/core/graph_client.py:_get_bytes_inner: the auth-refresh exception path emits a span with status_code=None when the bearer_token call itself raises. This is the right shape, but the path that follows the redirect-to-success-200 flow has no span specifically attributing the 200 to redirect_follows + auth_refreshes counters in metadata; consumers must look at retry_attempt and infer.
findings.resolution: Optional follow-up: include 'redirect_followed' and 'auth_refreshed' booleans in trace_graph_call metadata for the success span so dashboards can break down successful downloads by which retry paths fired.
